arguments.task: Research the official Consensus website and official first-party documentation for API, developer docs, authentication methods, pricing/access, rate limits, and any MCP/server/GitHub references. Open only official Consensus pages and clearly extract citation URLs for each finding. Also check whether there is a public API or developer platform, and whether access appears self-serve or gated. Return concise findings with source URLs.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Consensus sources.

raw_result.description: AI-powered scientific literature search platform with a public search API and MCP server for research workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Consensus API docs show authentication via x-api-key header for the REST API; the official MCP docs indicate OAuth login flows for clients such as Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing and API pages indicate the Consensus Search API is available upon approval / request access rather than fully self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs show at least a documented /v1/quick_search endpoint and API reference, but the full breadth of endpoints was not comprehensively verified from the gathered evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include an MCP page and official MCP server URL at mcp.consensus.app/mcp with setup guidance for Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears gated by approval/request-access despite public documentation; toolkit build is possible once access is granted or via the official MCP path.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Pricing evidence indicates Teams API usage is '$0.10 per request, rate limited' and Enterprise has custom pricing and limits; no precise technical rate-limit quotas were verified from the gathered docs.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official public API docs exist; official pricing explicitly mentions Search API availability; official MCP support is surfaced in docs and via a dedicated MCP endpoint, which is a strong agent-enablement signal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain ambiguous from the gathered evidence alone: exact access-tier semantics (approval-based vs sales-gated), complete API endpoint breadth, and whether OAuth applies only to MCP client login rather than the core API.
